title: dalea carthagenensis subsp. thyrsiflora
intro: Dalea carthagenensis subsp. thyrsiflora, commonly known as the Carthage or hairy prairie clover, is a perennial legume plant native to the Americas, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and distinctive hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Dalea carthagenensis subsp. thyrsiflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support wound healing, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to gastrointestinal issues.
